headline: Fortinet (FTNT) Stock After 94% YTD Gain Is The Rally Running Out Of Room
summary: If you are wondering whether Fortinet is still worth the price on your screen today or if the stock has already run too far, this article breaks down what the current share price might imply about value. Fortinet shares last closed at US$151.35, with total returns of 4.6% over 7 days, 18.3% over 30 days, 94.3% year to date, 46.8% over 1 year, 100.2% over 3 years, and 205.1% over 5 years. This performance puts recent valuation questions front and center for many investors. Recent news flow...

headline: Eli Lilly Leads 5 Stocks Near Buy Points As The Market Rally Rotates
summary: Eli Lilly and EZ Corp. lead stocks to watch near buy points this week as investors seek shelter in defensive growth names.  The relative strength lines of all top five stocks near buy points are making new highs or near highs.  The strong RS lines, which track performance vs. the S&P 500, are especially bullish given the overall uncertain stock market.

headline: Fortinet (FTNT) Launches FortiSOC And Adds Derek Kan To Its Board
summary: Fortinet (NasdaqGS:FTNT) has introduced FortiSOC, an AI powered, unified cloud security operations platform delivered as SaaS. The company is consolidating threat detection, incident response and related security operations workflows into a single offering aimed at enterprise and public sector customers. Fortinet has also appointed Derek Kan, a technology executive with experience in scaling businesses and deploying AI, to its board of directors. Fortinet is best known for its firewall,...

headline: The Real Risk Inside Cisco Stock
summary: After a strong run, it’s natural for any Cisco Systems (CSCO) shareholder to feel confident. The stock is up 72% over the last year, powered by a story of transformation. The company is capturing surging AI demand, raising its forecast for AI infrastructure orders to $9 billion for the fiscal year. But when a stock has priced in this much success, the risks change. The question is no longer just about whether the company can grow, but whether it can grow in a way that justifies the new, higher e

headline: The Old-School Signal That Lit Up Palo Alto Networks Stock
summary: While all eyes were on a futuristic AI platform, the most telling clue to the stock's takeoff lay buried in the company's oldest business.
